title: Signals

jokes.text: Yeah, you were the best thing ... I was the best thing that ever happened to you and you just threw it away.
jokes.setup: Selina is on the phone with her ex-husband Andrew, trying to deliver a cutting insult
jokes.punchline: She accidentally compliments him first, then corrects herself mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's flustered state and inability to deliver a clean insult. The self-correction reveals her emotional state.

jokes.text: And what about poor Memaw, who's guaranteed to be dead by March? ( quietly ) With any luck.
jokes.setup: Selina mentions her mother's mortality as part of a guilt trip about Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: She quietly adds her hope that her mother will actually die soon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that reveals character while being shockingly funny. The quiet delivery makes it even more cutting.

jokes.text: Yeah, I hope we have a number for a contract killer, too. ( laughs ) I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Selina asks for Advil for her headache after the Andrew call
jokes.punchline: She escalates to wanting a contract killer, Gary cheerfully volunteers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character work with Gary's eager willingness to commit murder for Selina.

jokes.text: Not quite. Like your mother, it's been previously loved and paid for by a couple of guys.
jokes.setup: Mike compliments Dan's new car
jokes.punchline: Dan delivers a brutal comeback about Mike's mother being a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Well-structured insult with good parallel construction between car and mother. Classic Dan cruelty.

jokes.text: Oh, the old 'drink your way out of alcoholism' approach. I like it.
jokes.setup: Mike explains he's added car debt to his existing debt
jokes.punchline: Dan compares it to drinking your way out of alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about faulty logic with a memorable phrase structure.

jokes.text: Not a problem. Uh, an issue. Not even an issue. A hitch. You had a drinking hitch?
jokes.setup: Dan asks about Mike's drinking problem
jokes.punchline: Mike keeps downgrading the severity, ending with the absurd 'drinking hitch'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's denial and Dan's incredulous response to 'drinking hitch.'

jokes.text: I get that all the time. - No, you don't.
jokes.setup: An aide politely dismisses Mike after he hasn't read important papers
jokes.punchline: Mike claims this happens all the time, Dan immediately contradicts him
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character beat showing Mike's delusion and Dan's bluntness.

jokes.text: But I need to talk to that autistic lumberjack son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Selina needs to talk to Kent
jokes.punchline: She describes him with this bizarre, specific insult combination
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and bizarre insult that captures both Kent's personality and Selina's frustration.

jokes.text: Hey, I heard you were doing Pilates. I didn't know you had a core. ( laughs ) I do and it's like steel.
jokes.setup: Selina makes a joke about Kent not having a core
jokes.punchline: Kent takes it literally and proudly describes his steel core
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent moment - missing the insult and responding with robotic literalness.

jokes.text: Yeah? What are we doing? - Nothing. Until the numbers support intervention, doing nothing is the most positive thing we can do.
jokes.setup: Selina asks what they're doing about the hostages
jokes.punchline: Kent explains that doing nothing is the most positive action
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic doublespeak that perfectly captures Kent's character and political absurdity.

jokes.text: You know, it's just so great talking to you, Kent. I love being close to the inaction.
jokes.setup: Kent's explanation about doing nothing being positive
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically says she loves being close to the 'inaction'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'action' and sharp sarcasm that fits Selina's character perfectly.

jokes.text: SM-17d. There's a lower case D. - Ah, apologies, sir. - Details are important. A misplaced decimal point can kill a diabetic.
jokes.setup: Kent corrects a minor detail about a missile system designation
jokes.punchline: He justifies this pedantry with the bizarre diabetic example
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent character comedy - obsessive attention to detail justified with completely unrelated medical example.

jokes.text: You know what? Why don't you put on your running shoes and get to the fucking point, Jonah?
jokes.setup: Jonah is taking too long to explain something
jokes.punchline: Selina tells him to put on running shoes to get to the point
jokes.explanation: Creative way to tell someone to hurry up, fits Selina's impatient character.

jokes.text: Sounds classy. Will Kate Middleton be there?
jokes.setup: Jonah mentions a 'pig picking' event
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically asks if royalty will attend this lowbrow event
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic contrast between high and low culture, typical Selina snobbery.

jokes.text: Okay, so they want me to go to a pig roast to meet a bunch of men who probably took turns to fuck the pig before they roasted it? I wouldn't presume they took turns.
jokes.setup: Selina's disgusted reaction to the pig picking event
jokes.punchline: Jonah's deadpan correction that he wouldn't presume they took turns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from Selina's crude assumption to Jonah's hilariously literal correction about the logistics.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. I'm going to that 4:30 meeting even if I have to get Sue to fly around the world the wrong way like Superman and reverse time.
jokes.setup: Selina insists she'll make the important meeting
jokes.punchline: She references Superman flying backwards to reverse time as a solution
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reference showing Selina's desperation and pop culture knowledge.

jokes.text: I'll bring you back a sandwich and a six-toed husband.
jokes.setup: Jonah asks to skip the pig picking to visit his sick father
jokes.punchline: Selina offers to bring him back rural stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Clever regional stereotype joke with good economy, though relies on cheap rural tropes.

jokes.text: It's just typing into a calendar. Oh ... !,I'm sorry, Sue. Your job is really difficult. - Excuse me? - You do it very efficiently.
jokes.setup: Gary dismisses Sue's work as just typing into a calendar
jokes.punchline: His attempt to backtrack makes it worse by being condescending
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Gary's social ineptitude and making things worse.

jokes.text: Okay, well, let's see, I'd rather set fire to my vulva.
jokes.setup: Amy suggests meeting with Congressman Furlong
jokes.punchline: Selina would rather commit genital self-harm
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Selina - graphic, shocking, and efficiently expressing her disgust.

jokes.text: Roger Furlong trying to play nice is like Brando trying to play Annie. - That's a good one, Mike.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Furlong trying to be pleasant for a Senate run
jokes.punchline: Amy compares it to Brando playing Annie (from the musical)
jokes.explanation: Sharp comparison joke with good cultural reference contrast between masculine Brando and innocent Annie.

jokes.text: Did you know that I'm paying 65 grand a year to that school? They had better be putting gold on that popcorn instead of butter, right?
jokes.setup: Selina learns Catherine wrote a problematic film studies paper
jokes.punchline: She references the high tuition cost expecting gold-level treatment
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about expensive college costs with Selina's materialistic perspective.

jokes.text: This must be the first pet project of yours that didn't involve resorting to chloroform.
jokes.setup: Jonah mentions this is his pet project
jokes.punchline: Mike implies Jonah's other projects involved drugging people
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific insult implying Jonah is a predator, delivered with Mike's deadpan style.

jokes.text: If you kill his mother and then dress in her skin, that's technically illegal. Technically.
jokes.setup: Dan says he found a way to get to Kent Davison
jokes.punchline: Amy suggests murder and impersonation, with the caveat it's only 'technically' illegal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific suggestion delivered with perfect deadpan understatement on the legality.

jokes.text: Where do you live? - Nearby. Local ... to here.
jokes.setup: Kent asks where Dan lives at the Pilates studio
jokes.punchline: Dan's awkward, vague response reveals he's stalking Kent
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dan's desperation and poor lying skills.

jokes.text: What the hell do you do on Air Force One, rub your dick on the seats? I haven't actually been on Air Force One.
jokes.setup: Jonah is overly excited about being on Air Force Two
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude insult reveals Jonah hasn't been on Air Force One
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Jonah's lack of access while delivering a crude Selina insult.

jokes.text: Oh. And your boxers occasionally, I hope. I have a fresh pair every day now.
jokes.setup: Mike says he's changed his reaction to his financial problems
jokes.punchline: Selina asks about changing boxers, Mike proudly announces daily changes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's low standards and Selina's cutting observations.

jokes.text: Those signals took years. I can't just tear up the gestictionary and come up with new codes like that.
jokes.setup: Selina says they need new signals after the old ones were leaked
jokes.punchline: Gary uses the made-up word 'gestictionary' for their gesture dictionary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant portmanteau that perfectly captures Gary's pedantic nature and the absurdity of their signal system.

jokes.text: I don't want to get stuck talking pig shit with people who use hay as furniture.
jokes.setup: Selina needs an exit strategy for the pig roast
jokes.punchline: She describes the attendees as people who use hay as furniture
jokes.explanation: Good class-based humor showing Selina's snobbery with vivid rural imagery.

jokes.text: No, that's like I squished a bug and then I said, 'Oh, it's so cool I squished a bug.'
jokes.setup: Mike suggests clapping as a new signal
jokes.punchline: Selina explains why it would look weird with this specific scenario
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Selina's visual thinking about how gestures would look.

jokes.text: What if people think I have crabs in my eyebrows? How can you have crabs in your eyebrow?
jokes.setup: Mike suggests rubbing eyebrows as a signal
jokes.punchline: Selina worries about crabs, Gary questions the biology
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist exchange with Gary's innocent biological confusion.

jokes.text: Okay, Gary, when a man and a woman love each other very much and it's the man's birthday...
jokes.setup: Gary questions how you can have crabs in your eyebrows
jokes.punchline: Selina starts explaining with a parental sex-ed tone but implies oral sex
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the 'birds and bees' talk with adult implications, perfectly timed cutoff.

jokes.text: If Owen Wilson's not in it, I probably don't know it. That doesn't sound like one of Owen's.
jokes.setup: Discussion of a serious Palestinian documentary
jokes.punchline: Mike's comment that it doesn't sound like an Owen Wilson movie
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Mike's limited film knowledge and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: This needs to be the Gettysburg Address of tightrope-walking, say-nothing bullshit.
jokes.setup: They need a careful statement about Catherine's essay
jokes.punchline: Selina describes it as the Gettysburg Address of meaningless political speak
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of Lincoln's greatest speech with political meaninglessness. Highly quotable and perfectly structured.

jokes.text: Well, howdy, y'all. Oh, my goodness. Look at this. Walking the walk, talking the pork.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to connect with rural voters
jokes.punchline: Her painfully forced folksy persona and mangled idiom
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe as Selina attempts to be folksy and fails spectacularly

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, go shit yourself, Long Tall Sally, because I will deal with my daughter in my own way on my own terms.
jokes.setup: Jonah demands Catherine apologize
jokes.punchline: Selina delivers a crude dismissal with the nickname 'Long Tall Sally'
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Selina's maternal protectiveness and creative insults for Jonah.

jokes.text: No, that came out wrong. Listen, how are you doing? Stop it. Stop it. You're freaking me out.
jokes.setup: Selina says something that sounds sexual about Mike and Catherine
jokes.punchline: Her flustered attempts to clarify make it worse
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Selina's discomfort with accidental innuendo.

jokes.text: My boyfriend in college was a quarter Cherokee. Mom, I'm sorry, but I'm not gonna say sorry.
jokes.setup: Catherine asks if Selina has a problem with her Iranian boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Selina's weak defense followed by Catherine's firm refusal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's weak attempt at appearing tolerant and Catherine's backbone.

jokes.text: Well, we've had happier conversations. Like when I told her her grandpa had died. Oh, she didn't like him, huh? No, that's not what I meant.
jokes.setup: Selina says they've had happier conversations than this fight
jokes.punchline: Mike misunderstands that the death announcement was happier because Catherine disliked grandpa
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Mike's logical but inappropriate interpretation of family dynamics.

jokes.text: Get some towels. I may need to daughter board her.
jokes.setup: Selina wants Mike to bring Catherine to the air base
jokes.punchline: She references waterboarding but makes it family-specific
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay combining torture methods with parental discipline. Perfectly Selina.

jokes.text: I'm running out of nice juice to spray at these shit munchers.
jokes.setup: Furlong is trying to be pleasant for his political ambitions
jokes.punchline: He describes niceness as 'juice to spray' at 'shit munchers'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Furlong - trying to be nice while being utterly crude. The 'juice to spray' metaphor is brilliant.

jokes.text: Not only Ginger Rogers, but also Ginger Rogers. Nice joke construction, sir.
jokes.setup: Furlong sees Amy and Selina together
jokes.punchline: He makes a weak joke about two gingers, his aide compliments the 'construction'
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about bad political humor and sycophantic aides praising terrible jokes.

jokes.text: Stop trying to polish my dick, you fucking four-eyed failure.
jokes.setup: His aide keeps complimenting his jokes
jokes.punchline: Furlong's crude dismissal of the sycophancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Furlong character moment - crude but self-aware about flattery.

jokes.text: You know, you're about as annoying as a condom filled with fire ants. How's that for a fucking metaphor? That's a simile, sir.
jokes.setup: Furlong creates an elaborate insult
jokes.punchline: His aide corrects that it's a simile, not a metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with vivid imagery followed by pedantic grammar correction that deflates Furlong's rage.

jokes.text: Mike, the VP is voicing solidarity with the Jewish people in front of rotating pork.
jokes.setup: Selina is giving pro-Israel statements at a pig roast
jokes.punchline: Kent points out the visual contradiction with clinical precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent observation about political/religious absurdity with clinical detachment. 'Rotating pork' is brilliant.

jokes.text: Most Jews in this country are fine with pork, Mr. Davison. Pork schmork, they often say.
jokes.setup: Kent is concerned about the pork/Jewish solidarity visual
jokes.punchline: Mike's confident but completely wrong assessment with made-up phrase
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's confident ignorance and the fake Yiddish phrase 'pork schmork.'

jokes.text: Son, that fucker is skinned and roasted. He's at a huge disadvantage.
jokes.setup: Jonah claims the pig is a security threat
jokes.punchline: Local man points out the pig is dead and cooked
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan logic pointing out the obvious absurdity of fearing a cooked pig.

jokes.text: Your wife is perfectly fine. She ran the Seattle Marathon. All right, don't fuck with me, honey.
jokes.setup: Furlong claims his wife was ill with kidney disease
jokes.punchline: Sue fact-checks him with the marathon, he drops the nice act
jokes.explanation: Excellent moment showing Sue's competence and Furlong's immediate personality switch when caught lying.

jokes.text: You can't raise pigs without excrement, ma'am. They defecate. And they defecate with speed. And volume.
jokes.setup: Farmer discusses environmental concerns about pig farming
jokes.punchline: His increasingly detailed and enthusiastic description of pig defecation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing farmer's pride in pig productivity even when discussing excrement.

jokes.text: I've been scratching my eyebrow for three and a half fucking hours.
jokes.setup: The established eyebrow signal from earlier
jokes.punchline: Selina's frustrated exaggeration of how long she's been signaling
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the signal discussion with Selina's typical impatience

jokes.text: I've been scratching my eyebrow for three and a half fucking hours. I thought we weren't doing the eyebrow thing. You said about the crabs.
jokes.setup: Selina has been using their agreed signal
jokes.punchline: Gary ignored it because of the earlier crab concern
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier signal discussion showing communication breakdown between Selina and Gary.

jokes.text: She's getting her leak on.
jokes.setup: Discussion of who leaked their secret signals
jokes.punchline: Gary uses inappropriate slang for leaking information
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Gary's attempt to use hip slang inappropriately.

jokes.text: Oh, dang, Amy. You put out quick. Right. It turns out I can't mate with anyone outside of my species.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Amy and Dan went on three dates
jokes.punchline: Amy compares Dan to a different species
jokes.explanation: Great Amy comeback using biological terms to insult Dan's humanity.

jokes.text: At work we actually call her Amiable Amy.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Amy being happy without children
jokes.punchline: Dan's clearly sarcastic nickname highlighting Amy's notorious unpleasantness
jokes.explanation: Good ironic nickname that everyone knows is the opposite of Amy's personality.

jokes.text: It's not 'Debbie Does Damascus.'
jokes.setup: Catherine defends her serious academic essay
jokes.punchline: She references the porn parody title format to show it's not frivolous
jokes.explanation: Clever porn parody reference showing Catherine's awareness while defending her academic work.

jokes.text: Sweet Jesus Christ. I want to see the film and I will see the film. Gary, we need to get it. It's 'Five Easy Pieces.' It's '5 Broken Cameras.'
jokes.setup: Mike mentions owing $100,000 in debt
jokes.punchline: Selina mishears/misremembers the film title as a Jack Nicholson movie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's cultural confusion and Gary's corrections.

jokes.text: This is gonna be like 'Super Size Me' all over again. Why don't you guys just go see 'Mamma Mia!' again for the 10th fucking time?
jokes.setup: Selina promises to watch Catherine's recommended film
jokes.punchline: Catherine knows they'll watch mainstream entertainment instead
jokes.explanation: Good generational/cultural clash with Catherine calling out her family's shallow viewing habits.

jokes.text: Ma'am, you need to make like a snake and get on that airplane.
jokes.setup: Mike needs to get Selina to leave for the security meeting
jokes.punchline: His mangled version of 'make like a tree and leave'
jokes.explanation: Good Mike moment showing his tendency to mangle common expressions while trying to be urgent.

jokes.text: Yeah, you know, I knew I should have gone with Amy Whitehouse.
jokes.setup: Amy criticizes the 'Amiable Amy' nickname
jokes.punchline: Dan's alternative reference to Amy Winehouse (who died of addiction)
jokes.explanation: Dark callback referencing the deceased singer, typical Dan cruelty but well-constructed.

jokes.text: Let's run a security check on him and his entire family. And investigate their finances.
jokes.setup: Selina meets Catherine's Iranian-American boyfriend
jokes.punchline: She immediately orders a full investigation of the nice college student
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's paranoia and abuse of power over her daughter's boyfriend.

jokes.text: Gary... I do not like to say this, but you have truly disappointed me today.
jokes.setup: Selina's reaction to Gary's signal leak
jokes.punchline: Her formal, parental disappointment tone
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the mother-son dynamic between Selina and Gary

jokes.text: I am having the best day. This is such a great time. We're on Air Force Two. U.S. Hey is getting started. Guys, we saved the Florida vote. You guys want to play iPad Scrabble?
jokes.setup: Everyone else is miserable after a disastrous day
jokes.punchline: Jonah is obliviously ecstatic about minor achievements
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah obliviousness contrasted with everyone else's misery, ending with childish game suggestion.

tmdb_overview: Selina attends a pig roast in North Carolina as part of the president's "Listen to Rural America" initiative. Dan takes up Pilates to get face time.
insight: Sixty-one jokes in 38 minutes: character chaos sustains momentum despite uneven punchlines.
